title: calamagrostis stricta subsp. borealis
intro: Calamagrostis stricta subsp. borealis, also known as northern fescue or boreal bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Calamagrostis stricta subsp. borealis include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
